hazard.summary: Anthropic Retry-After is honored by the provider-pool cooldown layer but overridden by lower-level claude-core.js retry handlers, causing call-level retries to fire inside the provider recovery window under concurrent load.
signals: claude-core.js non-streaming retry handler computes baseDelay * Math.pow(2, retryCount) without reading Retry-After header (lines 111-115)
claude-core.js streaming retry handler applies identical local exponential backoff (lines 207-212)
Maintainer confirmed: low-level retry handlers compute locally-based exponential backoff without reading Retry-After
Higher-level getRateLimitCooldownRecoveryTime in src/utils/common.js reads Retry-After correctly at pool level
Individual callApi/streamApi retries within pool cooldown window still use local backoff — can retry too early under concurrent load
RFC #580 defines reset time parsing priority: adapter-specific parser → generic header parsing → config default
PR #579 adds Reset hint parser preferring Retry-After header with fallback to local backoff
PR #578 adds quota-aware account switching — 429 responses classified as quota events not generic errors

impact.operator_impact: Operators may see persistent 429s or degraded Claude request throughput even though a pool-level cooldown exists, because lower-level retries can continue firing inside the recovery window under concurrent load.
impact.business_consequence: Concurrent retries inside Anthropic cooldown windows waste paid API attempts, extend recovery time, reduce successful throughput, and forced a quota management redesign across pool and call layers.

constraints: Retry-After must be treated as the minimum retry delay across all retry layers that can issue provider calls.
Lower-level retry handlers must not schedule retries earlier than higher-level provider-pool cooldown windows.
Rate-limit recovery timing must be coordinated between provider-pool cooldown logic and per-call retry logic.
429 quota events must not be handled as generic retryable errors when provider recovery timing is available.
A request path must have a single clear retry owner so local backoff cannot override provider-directed cooldown.
verification: Setup: Mock an Anthropic 429 with Retry-After longer than local exponential backoff. Action: Trigger claude-core.js non-streaming retry path. Assertion: Next retry delay equals or exceeds Retry-After-derived cooldown, not local backoff value.
Setup: Mock the same 429 in the streaming path. Action: Trigger streamApi retry. Assertion: Streaming retry also honors Retry-After before falling back to local backoff.

Setup: Enable pool cooldown and trigger 429 with Retry-After. Action: Run concurrent callApi/streamApi requests during pool recovery window. Assertion: No lower-level retry fires a provider call before pool-level cooldown expires.

Setup: Provide 429 quota response with adapter-specific reset hint, Retry-After header, and config default. Action: Pass through quota-aware management. Assertion: Reset timing follows priority — adapter-specific parser, then generic header, then config default.

Setup: Trigger 429 quota response after implementing quota-aware account switching. Action: Classify and route through provider pools. Assertion: Response classified as quota event, not generic error. Retry and account-switching behavior aligns with selected reset window.
notes: This receipt documents a layered retry-policy divergence. The finding was confirmed by the maintainer within 28 minutes and consolidated into a broader quota-aware management RFC (#580). The failure was not that Retry-After was absent everywhere — a higher-level provider-pool cooldown path already read it. The failure was that lower-level callApi/streamApi handlers retained independent local exponential backoff and could retry inside the provider recovery window. The maintainer response connected the fix to a unified reset hint parser (PR #579) and quota-aware account switching design (PR #578). This is the first receipt in the corpus where a Pitstop finding triggered an RFC-level architectural response rather than a single-file fix. max_probe_qps is set to 1 per the maintainer RFC design intent for limited_qps probe strategy.

constraints: Provider recovery timing must propagate consistently across pool and call retry layers.
Local retry policies must not override provider-directed cooldown windows.
Quota events must be classified before generic retry behavior runs.
anti_patterns: pool-level cooldown with independent call-level retries
multiple retry owners on the same provider request path
quota-bearing 429 treated as generic retryable error
